rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope
Use subcomponent ports only in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections are defined
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Use assign only in implementation scopes when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component structure from the input AADL model
Preserve the original AADL architecture during annex insertion
Use valid component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Use implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type scopes
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
